description_long: This object is a cluster of translucent, prismatic crystals with sharply defined edges and a glassy luster, resembling quartz or calcite. The main body consists of several elongated, angular points radiating outward from a common base, which is slightly rougher and more opaque with hints of brown mineral inclusions. The crystal faces are smooth and reflective, catching light and creating subtle internal reflections and refractions. The coloration is predominantly clear to milky white, with some areas appearing more transparent and others cloudier due to internal fractures or inclusions. The overall form is irregular and organic, with each crystal terminating in a pointed tip and the group forming a low, wide cluster. This type of stone is often found in natural settings and can serve as a decorative or natural obstacle in urban parks or landscaped areas.
description: A cluster of clear to milky white prismatic crystals with sharp, reflective faces and a rough, brownish base. The stone has an irregular, radiating form and a glassy, translucent appearance.
description_view_0: Three main crystal points radiate outward, showing smooth, reflective faces and a slightly rough, brownish base.
description_view_1: A narrow side profile reveals the thinness of the crystal cluster and the elongated, blade-like structure of the points.
description_view_2: The cluster is viewed from the opposite side, highlighting the angular arrangement and the semi-translucent quality of the crystals.
description_view_3: Another narrow side profile, similar to view 1, emphasizing the linear alignment and sharp edges of the crystals.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on gravel or soil
attribute_pattern: Natural prismatic pattern with angular, faceted faces and subtle internal veining; no artificial carvings or regular motifs.
attribute_weathering: Smooth and polished on crystal faces, with minor roughness and discoloration at the base indicating some exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with softer landscaping materials but can blend with other natural stones or gravel; may partially collect dust or debris at the base.
